detail: Feast-of-the-dead dream — Robb + Grey Wind bleeding at the door; foreshadow of Red Wedding
Flayed-man battle armor: sullen-red rounded helm, gorget shaped like a skinless face, pink cloak
Leaf first appearance: dappled skin, large gold-and-green slitted eyes, hair woven with vines
note: row tagged adwd-bran-02:135; species node used

detail: Wight description: pale flesh, black hands, eyes glowed like pale blue stars
Hodor frozen: icicles in beard, mustache a lump of frozen snot glittering in sunset light
Coldhands on Others: 'The white walkers go lightly on the snow. You'll find no prints to mark their passage'
Children physical: nut-brown dappled skin, large golden cat's eyes, three fingers + thumb with black claws
Bloodraven on weirwoods and time: 'a thousand human years are a moment to a weirwood'
Ned in Winterfell godswood seen through greensight; Bran's first weirwood vision
Robert Strong first appearance: eight feet tall, white enameled plate, gilded mail, greathelm hiding face
Wedding-day peace-longing: 'I want to plant my olive trees and see them fruit'
Wyman self-description: 'I am too fat to sit a horse… My body has become a prison more dire than the Wolf's Den'
Varys to dying Kevan: 'I bear you no ill will. This was not done from malice. It was for the realm.'
Maester Aemon: 'Kill the boy, Jon Snow. Kill the boy and let the man be born.'
Val at Mance's execution: 'she looked more regal in bronze than Stannis did in gold'
Night's Watch oath in full at the weirwood grove — book-cite overlay
Wun Wun dismembers Ser Patrek — head 'red and pulpy as a summer melon'
Mance-as-Rattleshirt physical: widow's peak, close-set dark eyes, worm-mustache, broken brown teeth
Three rangers' severed heads on ash spears, beards full of ice, empty eye-sockets — the Weeper's work
Sleeve-powders catalogued: powders to turn fire green/blue/silver, smoke for truth/lust/fear, thick black smoke to kill
Fire-vision: 'Skulls. A thousand skulls, and the bastard boy again. Jon Snow.' — foreshadowing Jon's assassination
Ramsay's fullest physical description: big-boned, pink blotchy skin, ghost-grey eyes like dirty ice, garnet blood-drop eardrop
The Reek mantra: 'My name is Reek. It rhymes with leek'
Roose Bolton appearance: smooth ageless, lips vanish, 'on Roose Bolton's face, rage and joy looked much the same. His eyes are ice.' — DRIFT: route cited :89, verified :89
JonCon's core wound: Rhaegar at Griffin's Roost — 'Your father's lands are beautiful'
Aegon gives Duck the white cloak over JonCon's objection: 'Duck will die for me if need be'
Barristan in Dany's gift armor: 'hard as ice and bright as new-fallen snow'
Hair freshly dyed deep dark blue — already in node
Black Balaq: white-haired Summer Islander, skin dark as soot, feathered cloak of green and orange
Gorys Edoryen: leopard skin, blood-red hair in oiled ringlets, pointed black beard
Lysono Maar: Lyseni, lilac eyes, white-gold hair, purple fingernails, earlobes dripping pearls
Homeless Harry soaking blistered feet in salt water throughout the war council — reluctant captain-general
JonCon's death-clock: 'I still have time. A year. Two years. Five... Time enough to see Griffin's Roost again'
JonCon RESENTS Varys: 'Let me live long enough to see the boy sit the Iron Throne, and Varys will pay'
Bittersteel's gilded skull on a tall pike: commanded his men to boil the flesh and dip skull in gold
Jeyne in bridal dress: white lambswool + freshwater pearls, 'Her face was pale, bloodless. A face carved of ice.'
Ruined snowbound Winterfell description — belongs in locations/winterfell.node.md, not a character/species node
Wyman eats six portions from the three pies, smacking lips, gravy stains — performed gluttony as cover
Wyman's feast-performance: laughing and slapping lords on back, calling for tunes — fake joy as mask
Lady Dustin's ironic misread: 'Have you ever seen a fat man so happy? He is almost dancing'
Ramsay 'hacked off slices with his falchion' at the feast — physicality of the Frey-pie cutting
Theon's moral self-inventory: 'I have done terrible things... but I am no kinslayer'
Young Griff's eyes (Tyrion POV): black by lamplight, purple at dusk, eyelashes 'as long as any woman's'
Alayne's disguise instability: 'changed her own rich auburn into Alayne's burnt brown, but seldom long before the red began creeping back at the roots'
Mya Stone in silvery ringmail: 'black as a raven's wing,' big blue eyes — 'those are his eyes, and she has his hair' (Robert provenance confirmed)
Ghost wolf foreshadowing on the high saddle: 'It sounds like a wolf, thought Sansa. A ghost wolf, big as mountains'
Broken men sermon: 'Almost all are common-born... war and pestilence made them what they are'
'Wolves are nobler than that... and so are dogs, I think' — book-cite overlay added; cross-ref also added to sandor-clegane node
Gendry's Robert-build (Brienne sees Renly first): heavy shoulders, muscular right arm, black hair, blue eyes
'Sapphires,' she whispered at him, as she gave her blade a hard twist that made him shudder
Meribald's great she-wolf pack on the Trident 'known to bring aurochs down all by herself' — almost certainly Nymeria's pack — DRIFT: route cited brienne-07:259, correct is brienne-05:259
Lady Stoneheart revealed: bronze circlet, 'dry brittle white hair,' mottled green-grey flesh in ragged strips from eyes to jaw
First AFFC valonqar beat: 'It is blood I need, not water. Tyrion's blood, the blood of the valonqar.'
Citadel expulsion: 'I wished to understand the nature of death, so I opened the bodies of the living'
First description of the High Sparrow scrubbing the floor: grey-brown beard, hair in hard knot, bare calloused feet — book-cite overlay
Foreshadowing — Cersei's internal thought 'I will deal with this one as I did the last' re High Sparrow; foreshadows her disastrous miscalculation with the Faith
'Short, squat, and warty, with pebbly greenish jowls. Her teeth were gone and her dugs hung down to her knees' — book-cite overlay
Tent interior: cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, saffron; 'a dim green light' from iron basilisk-head brazier — book-cite overlay
Nightmare valonqar: 'The valonqar shall wrap his hands about your throat... fingers dug deep into her neck'
Blue Bard all-blue wardrobe: supple calfskin boots, fine wool breeches, silk slashed with satin, Tyroshi blue-dyed hair — book-cite overlay added to existing wiki quote
Qyburn torturing the Blue Bard: 'a wet red eye wept blood... Lord Tywin would not have turned away'
'No golden shrouds, no valonqar, I am free of your croaking malice at last' — already in node at line 241 with book cite
Marillion post-torture: bathed, sky-blue + white tunic, white silk gloves over ruined hands, white silk bandage over burned-out eyes
Mord gold-teeth reveal: 'Sansa saw to her astonishment that the gaoler's teeth were made of gold'
Petyr/Littlefinger duality monologue: 'He was Petyr, her protector... but he was also Littlefinger... And Littlefinger was no friend of hers' — DRIFT: route cited :117, correct is :69
Long Night description: 'Fear is for the long night, when the sun hides its face for years at a time' — book-cite overlay added to existing wiki quote
Throne deliberation: Jaime briefly tempted to proclaim Viserys king before sitting the Iron Throne himself
Aerys's fire-rebirth delusion: 'he would rise again, reborn as a dragon, and turn all his enemies to ash'
Identity-arc: 'The hand that made me Kingslayer. The goat had robbed him of his glory and his shame... Who am I now?'
'It's not Aerys I rue, it's Robert... Why is it that no one names Robert oathbreaker?'
'By what right does the wolf judge the lion? By what right?' — already in node at line 193 with book cite
'Jaime, he thought, my name is Jaime.' — identity-reclamation after Brienne shouts 'The Kingslayer!'
White Book entry verbatim: 'slew King Aerys II at the foot of the Iron Throne. Thereafter known as the Kingslayer.'
LF doctrine: 'Always keep your foes confused... Sometimes the best way to baffle them is to make moves that have no purpose' — book-cite overlay added to existing node quote
'In a better world, you might have been mine, not Eddard Stark's. My loyal loving daughter' — new quote added
Moon-tea confession: 'they murdered him with moon tea, with tansy and mint and wormwood... I only drank what Father gave me' — book-cite overlay added to existing wiki quote
